project.title: PtCaf1 interacted proteins in EV and PtGTSF1 KD of Paramecium tetraurelia
project.projectDescription: The DNA fragment of PtCAF1, one of the core component of PRC2, is fused with FlagHA and transformed into the macronuclear of Paramecium tetraurelia. The interacted proteins of PtCaf1 in Empty vector (EV) control and PtGTSF1 silenced cells were immunoprecipitated with anti-HA antibody, followed with mass spectrometry.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: 1.1 Proteolysis (1) Soaked the gel in 50μL of pure water for 30 minutes, then took out and cut (1-2 mm gel particles, if the gel particles meet the size of 1-2 mm, then skip the process) and transferred to the corresponding EP tube; (2) Added 1mL of decolorizing solution (50mM NH4HCO3: acetonitrile = 1:1, V:V), vortexed for 10 seconds, decolorized at 37°C for 30 minutes, briefly centrifuged and dried; repeated several times until the blue faded (Silver dyed gel does not need to be decolored, it can be washed directly with 1mL of pure water for 3 times); (3) Added 500μL of acetonitrile to dehydrate the gel to completely whiten, repeat once; soaked up the acetonitrile, opened the centrifuge tube and placed it in a clean bench for 10 minutes to air-dry acetonitrile, add 10mM DTT (1M DTT: 25mM NH4HCO3=1:100) until the liquid covered the gel, then put in water bath at 56°C for 1 hour; after cooling to room temperature, soaked it up, added 55mM IAM (0.55 M IAM:25mM NH4HCO3 = 1:10) quickly until the liquid covered the gel, and put at room temperature in a dark room for 45 minutes; (4) Soaked up the liquid, washed twice with 500μL of decolorizing solution (50 mM NH4HCO3: acetonitrile = 1:1, V:V), washed once with pure water; soaked up the liquid, added 500μL of acetonitrile, vortexed for 5 minutes. Soaked up the acetonitrile, opened the centrifuge tube lid and placed it on the clean bench for 10 minutes to dry thoroughly; (5) Diluted the enzyme concentration to 0.01μg/μL with 25mM NH4HCO3, and completely covered the gel with the enzyme solution, and stood at 4°C or ice for 30 minutes. After the gel was swelled, added the corresponding buffer to the gel. Incubated overnight at 37°C. (6) Added 5 times volume of 50% ACN the next day, vortexed for 5 minutes, centrifuged at 5,000g for 1 minute, transferred the supernatant to the new centrifuge tubes of the same number; added 5 volumes of 100% ACN, vortexed for 5 minutes. After centrifugation at 5,000g for 1 minute, the supernatant was transferred to a new centrifuge tube of the same number. Finally, the resulting supernatant was centrifuged at 25,000g for 5 minutes, and the supernatant was taken for freeze-dried. 1.2 HPLC The dried peptide samples were reconstituted with mobile phase A (2% ACN, 0.1% FA), centrifuged at 20,000g for 10 minutes, and the supernatant was taken for injection. Separation was performed by Thermo UltiMate 3000 UHPLC. The sample was first enriched in trap column and desalted, and then entered a self-packed C18 column (75μm internal diameter, 3μm column size, 25cm column length) and separated at a flow rate of 300nL/min by the following effective gradient: 0~5 minutes, 5% mobile phase B (98% ACN, 0.1% FA); 5~45 minutes, mobile phase B linearly increased from 5% to 25%; 45~50minutes, mobile phase B increased from 25% to 35%; 50~52 minutes, mobile phase B rose from 35% to 80%; 52~54 minutes, 80% mobile phase B; 54~60 minutes, 5% mobile phase B. The nanoliter liquid phase separation end was directly connected to the mass spectrometer. 1.3 Mass Spectrometry Detection The peptides separated by liquid phase chromatography were ionized by a nanoESI source and then passed to a tandem mass spectrometer Orbitrap Fusion Lumos (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA) for DDA (Data Dependent Acquisition) mode detection. The main parameters were set: ion source voltage was set to 2kV, MS1 mass spectrometer scanning range was 350~1,500m/z; resolution was set to 60,000; MS2 starting m/z was fixed at 100; resolution was 15,000. The ion screening conditions for MS2 fragmentation: charge 2+ to 6+, aynd the top 30 parent ions with the peak intensity exceeding 20,000. The ion fragmentation mode was HCD, and the fragment ions were detected in Orbitrap. The dynamic exclusion time was set to 30 seconds. The AGC was set to: MS1 1E5, MS2 2E4.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The results generated by the search engine are processed by MaxQuant with the parameters set as follows: the enzyme was specified as trypsin; the missed cleavage was set to 2; the minimum peptide length was 7; the fixed modification was cysteine carbamidomethyl (C); the variable modifications included Oxidation (M) and Acetyl (Protein N-term); LFQ of label-free quantification was selected

references.referenceLine: Wang C, Lyv L, Solberg T, Zhang H, Wen Z, Gao F. GTSF1 is required for transposon silencing in the unicellular eukaryote Paramecium tetraurelia. Nucleic Acids Res. 2024 52(21):13206-13223
